Patients on long-term treatment with warfarin should be advised to consult their physician if they experience a minor trauma or injury, or if there is a change in diet or exercise routine. Patients who require surgery or anesthesia should be warned of the increased risk of bleeding associated with warfarin therapy. The dose of warfarin should be increased when the patient undergoes surgery or anesthesia. The dosage of warfarin should be increased by 25% to 50%. These increases were associated with higher serum concentrations of dapsone and tetracyclines. It is recommended that patients receiving dapsone and potent CYP3A4 inhibitors have serum dapsone levels monitored more frequently than those receiving dapsone alone.
